meta.description: Identify robust molecular subtypes via consensus clustering (ConsensusClusterPlus). Inputs: expression matrix. Outputs: optimal k selection plot, consensus matrix heatmaps, subtype membership table, silhouette scores.

functional_suitability.note: Dependency installation steps are not documented for first-run users.

reliability.note: Recovery is strong, but there is no resumable checkpoint after partial method execution.

agent_usability.note: Execution mode is implied by examples rather than explicitly labeled near the top.

key_strengths: Strong progressive disclosure keeps the main skill concise while deeper details live in references and modular scripts.
Input validation is robust and backed by a clear SKILL_* error taxonomy.
Reproducibility is strong through seed control, session capture, and deterministic reruns.
Engineering quality is high: modular R files, passing automated tests, and clean output contracts.

recommendations.title: Add first-run dependency setup
recommendations.problem: First-time users only learn about required packages after hitting a runtime dependency error.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill documents runtime usage well, but omits an upfront installation block.
recommendations.fix: Add a short setup section near the top of SKILL.md with install commands for optparse, data.table, and ConsensusClusterPlus. Keep it minimal and place it before the main usage example.

recommendations.title: Label the execution mode explicitly
recommendations.problem: The skill is clearly runnable, but external routers or auditors may need to infer that it is an R CLI skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Execution mode is shown through examples rather than stated directly in a dedicated line.
recommendations.fix: Add one explicit sentence near the top that this skill is invoked with Rscript scripts/main.R. This improves routing clarity without changing behavior.

recommendations.title: Document dirty-data boundaries
recommendations.problem: The skill validates core format issues well, but its documentation does not spell out expectations for NA values, missing sample overlap, or batch-effect preprocessing.
recommendations.root_cause: Current guidance focuses on the happy path and a small set of common errors.
recommendations.fix: Add a short preprocessing boundary section describing unsupported dirty-data conditions and when users should clean data before running the skill.

recommendations.title: Expand regression coverage for failure modes
recommendations.problem: The current tests cover smoke runs and one invalid custom-gene case, but not several other negative-path validations.
recommendations.root_cause: Automated coverage is strongest for successful CLI flows and lighter for malformed inputs.
recommendations.fix: Add tests for NA or non-numeric expression matrices, missing sample overlap, and unsupported input extensions. This will lock in the current validation contract more completely.
